description_long: This is a classic submachine gun with a robust black metal receiver and barrel, complemented by polished wooden furniture on the stock, foregrip, and pistol grip. The rifle features a fixed wooden buttstock and a horizontal foregrip, both with a rich brown finish and visible wood grain. The barrel is long and slender, with a ribbed section near the receiver and a simple front sight post. The magazine is a detachable box type, inserted vertically in front of the trigger guard. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the receiver and barrel, providing basic aiming capability. The overall design is utilitarian, with a blend of metal and wood materials typical of early to mid-20th-century firearms.
description: A classic submachine gun with a black metal body and polished wooden stock and grips, featuring a long barrel, iron sights, and a detachable box magazine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, with the wooden stock, foregrip, and pistol grip clearly visible, as well as the magazine and iron sights.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel's end, front sight post, and the narrow profile of the rifle, with the wooden foregrip and stock edges visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rifle's length, wooden components, and the magazine, mirroring the details seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the wooden stock and the alignment of the receiver and barrel, with the iron sights visible along the top.
